canonical_definition: Evaluation metric for unconditional text generation that approximates the Kullback-Leibler divergence between model and data distributions using a large language model as a proxy for the true data distribution.
papers: Latent Thought Models with Variational Bayes Inference-Time Computation

relationships.evidence: In order to quantify sample quality, we use “generative perplexity”, a metric that computes the likelihood of generated samples under a more capable model... (Sec. 5.3)
